input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check what's available
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install latest .NET 8 SDK (or 9, adjust as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (via MAUI workload or manually)

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically. To check/manage them:

```powershell
# Find where Android SDK was installed
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check environment variable
$env:ANDROID_HOME

# List installed SDK packages
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific platform/build-tools if needed
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 4. Install Windows App SDK (for WinUI3/Windows target)

```powershell
# Usually handled by the MAUI workload, but to confirm:
winget search "Windows App SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK

# Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows deployment)
Start-Process ms-settings:developers
```

## 5. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Build Tools

If you want CLI-only (no full VS):

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via the installer or:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.XamarinBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet
```

## 6. Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install a system image
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V is enabled (required for emulator acceleration)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All
# Enable if needed (requires reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All
```

## 7. Create & Run a MAUI Project

```powershell
# See available templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create project
dotnet new maui -n MyApp
cd MyApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. Discover Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# Latest .NET SDK version
dotnet --version

# Available target frameworks
dotnet new maui --list-frameworks   # or check the .csproj TargetFrameworks

# Installed Android platforms
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Windows SDK versions on system
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" | Select-Object Name

# Check MAUI workload version
dotnet workload list --versions
```

---

**Key notes:**
- .NET 8+ is recommended (LTS); adjust `net8.0` to `net9.0` if using .NET 9.
- The `maui` workload pulls in Android + WinUI dependencies automatically.
- Windows target requires **Developer Mode** enabled and a Windows 10 SDK.
- Android emulator needs **Hyper-V** or **WHPX** for hardware acceleration.
